chunks.text: My father, who was born and raised in a fishing village
has lived with the sea.
He would dare to take the boat out even on stormy days and support the family.
When my father was young, fish were abundant.
In the morning fishing, the nets would pile with silver fish.
Dealer waited at the port, and prices were set by auction.
But over the decades, the situation changed greatly.
The amount of fish in the sea kept decreasing.
The causes are complex.
Overfishing, ocean pollution, climate change.
At first, my father insisted that " the old methods are enough.
" His feeling to preserve the conventional way of fishing was strong.
But the change of the age cannot be resisted.
I, his son
introduced my father to new technology — fish finders and medium - sized seine nets.
My father, at first, resisted this " mechanization.
" But once he tried it, he was surprised at the efficiency.
" Fishing too must update," he came to say with a laugh.
But efficiency alone is not sustainable.
At the same time
we reserved closed fishing zones and made sure not to take too much.
Based on relevant scientific proof, we investigate the fish population and aim for sustainable fishing.
My father now passes his experience on to younger fishermen.
So that the bounty of the sea reaches the generation of our children and grandchildren.
That is the shared wish of my father and me.
